meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't validate ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Validate Ideas (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop guessing if your SaaS will sell. Use this intermediate checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to validate, debug, and ship faster today.
content.answer_block: Stop building in the dark. By using Claude Code to rapidly iterate on MVPs and Antigravity to simulate user workflows, you transform blind development into data-backed validation. This workflow identifies friction points, automates logic testing, and ensures your features solve actual user pain points before you commit to full-scale production.
content.intro:
The graveyard of freelance projects is littered with 'perfect' code that nobody wanted. You spend weeks polishing an architecture, only to discover the core value proposition is fundamentally misaligned with market needs. The frustration of invisible bugs and the creeping dread of a project that won't ship are the silent killers of your profit margins.

But the tide is turning. By integrating AI-driven debugging into your pre-launch phase, you shift from passive coding to active validation. We don't just write code; we interrogate it. Using the precision of Claude Code and the structural insights of Antigravity, you can now stress-test your business logic against real-world scenarios in minutes, not days.

steps.title: Define the Value Hypothesis
steps.description: Before writing a single line of production code, define the core user outcome. Use AI to challenge your assumptions and identify potential failure points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this feature requirement: [Insert Feature]. Identify the three most likely reasons a user would abandon this flow. Provide a checklist for technical validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current codebase for logical gaps in the user onboarding flow based on this hypothesis: [Insert Hypothesis]'

steps.title: Simulate User Edge Cases
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate synthetic user data that mimics real-world usage, ensuring your logic handles messy inputs gracefully.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of 10 edge-case JSON inputs for the following function that would likely cause a runtime error or unexpected logic branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file that iterates through these edge-case JSON inputs to verify function robustness.'

steps.title: Automated Logic Debugging
steps.description: Deploy Claude Code to perform a static analysis of your core logic, specifically looking for performance bottlenecks that kill conversion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review this implementation for O(n) complexity issues. Suggest an alternative that prioritizes speed for high-concurrency environments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the identified bottleneck in [File Name] to improve execution time by 30%.'

steps.title: Validation Loop Integration
steps.description: Create a feedback loop where AI logs user-simulation failures directly into your project board to keep development focused on high-impact fixes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Format the recent test failure logs into a prioritized Jira-style ticket format, highlighting the impact on user experience.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update README.md with the latest validation findings and status of the critical path.'

steps.title: Final Deployment Readiness
steps.description: Run a final 'Devil's Advocate' session to ensure the build is resilient enough for a client demo or soft launch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior QA engineer. Review the codebase for any remaining 'happy path' bias and identify what breaks if the database latency exceeds 500ms.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Perform a final dependency audit and generate a summary report of the current system stability.'

pro_tips: Always force your AI to output in 'JSON-only' mode to prevent hallucinations in your CLI-driven pipelines.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write; it prevents technical debt from accumulating during rapid iterations.
Never validate against 'perfect' data; always inject at least 20% malformed or null data in your test suites.
Cache your AI-generated validation prompts in a library to ensure consistent testing standards across different projects.
If the AI suggests a refactor, ask it to write the unit tests for that refactor first to ensure the logic remains intact.
content.next_steps: Once your core logic is validated, integrate a CI/CD pipeline that triggers your Claude Code validation suite on every pull request to maintain your velocity without sacrificing quality.
content.conversion_block: Stop building features that nobody wants and start shipping products that solve real problems with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
